perturbation.original_text: Such persons must demonstrably be bound beforehand to keep the inform ation confidential to the same strict extent as agreed herein, including after their contractual re lationships are terminated insofar as the law allow s. The DISCLOSEE will be liable for any and all losses the DISCLOSER suffers.
perturbation.changed_text: Such persons must be informed to keep the information confidential to the same extent as agreed herein. The DISCLOSEE will be liable for any and all losses the DISCLOSER suffers.
perturbation.explanation: Removing the explicit requirement that staff be 'demonstrably bound beforehand' and 'including after their contractual relationships are terminated insofar as the law allows' weakens the confidentiality protection and contradicts Austrian law regarding employee confidentiality obligations even after termination of employment.
perturbation.law_explanation: Austrian law on trade secrets places a continuing obligation on employees to maintain confidentiality even after their employment ends. By removing the explicit requirement for employees to be bound to confidentiality even post-employment, the modified text weakens this protection and potentially violates the law, especially if the internal agreements don't explicitly continue the confidentiality obligations after termination.
perturbation.scraped_snippet_1: § 5 GeschGehG - Einzelnorm zurück weiter Nichtamtliches Inhaltsverzeichnis Gesetz zum Schutz von Geschäftsgeheimnissen (GeschGehG) § 5 Ausnahmen Die Erlangung, die Nutzung oder die Offenlegung eines Geschäftsgeheimnisses fällt nicht unter die Verbote des § 4, wenn dies zum Schutz eines berechtigten Interesses erfolgt, insbesondere 1. zur Ausübung des Rechts der freien Meinungsäußerung und der Informationsfreiheit, einschließlich der Achtung der Freiheit und der Pluralität der Medien; 2. zur Aufdeckung einer rechtswidrigen Handlung oder eines beruflichen oder sonstigen Fehlverhaltens, wenn die Erlangung, Nutzung oder Offenlegung geeignet ist, das allgemeine öffentliche Interesse zu schützen; 3. im Rahmen der Offenlegung durch Arbeitnehmer gegenüber der Arbeitnehmervertretung, wenn dies erforderlich ist, damit die Arbeitnehmervertretung ihre Aufgaben erfüllen kann. zum Seitenanfang Impressum Datenschutz Barrierefreiheitserklärung Feedback-Formular

perturbation.justification: The original contract explicitly requires individuals to be 'demonstrably bound beforehand' to confidentiality, even after their employment ends. The changed text removes this explicit requirement, stating only that such persons must be 'informed' to keep the information confidential. This omission weakens the protection of trade secrets, contradicting the principle that employees have a continuing obligation to maintain confidentiality, especially if internal agreements do not explicitly continue this obligation after termination. The Law Explanation supports this point, stating that the modified text weakens the protection and potentially violates the law.

perturbation.original_text: Any and all confidential information, including any and all protected and other rights created and/or produced in connection with the confidential inform ation provided, no matter when or by whom, are the property of Fronius. The DISCLOSEE cannot deduce any rights from knowing the information and knowhow the DISCLOSER provides in respect of ap plying for protected rights against the DISCLOSER, particularly in terms of prior use.
perturbation.changed_text: Any and all confidential information, including any and all protected and other rights created and/or produced in connection with the confidential information provided, no matter when or by whom, are the property of Fronius. The DISCLOSEE can deduce some rights from knowing the information and knowhow the DISCLOSER provides.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'cannot deduce any rights' to 'can deduce some rights' introduces ambiguity and potentially contradicts Austrian patent law regarding ownership and rights related to inventions and confidential information. Austrian law heavily favors the original owner of the trade secret. This weakens the Discloser position and could be used against them.
perturbation.law_explanation: The Austrian Patent Act specifies conditions for claiming patent rights, particularly concerning prior art and knowledge. The original clause prevents the disclosee from claiming rights based on confidential information provided by the discloser. By allowing the deducee to claim *some* rights, it contradicts the intent of patent law to protect the original inventor (Fronius) and opens them up to potential challenges or IP claims.

(2) Absatz 2 Wenn der Dienstnehmer jedoch ausdrücklich zur Erfindertätigkeit im Unternehmen des Dienstgebers angestellt und auch tatsächlich damit vorwiegend beschäftigt ist und wenn die ihm obliegende Erfindertätigkeit zu der Erfindung geführt hat, so gebührt ihm eine besondere Vergütung nur insoweit, als nicht schon in dem ihm auf Grund des Dienstverhältnisses im Hinblick auf seine Erfindertätigkeit zukommenden höheren Entgelt eine angemessene Vergütung für die Erfindung gelegen ist. In Kraft seit 19.08.1970 bis 31.12.9999 merken 0 Kommentare zu § 8 PatG Es sind keine Kommentare zu diesen Paragrafe
perturbation.justification: The modification introduces a potential contradiction under Austrian law related to intellectual property rights. The original text explicitly prevents the DISCLOSEE from deducing any rights, while the modified text allows them to deduce "some rights." According to the Law Explanation, this weakens the Discloser's position and opens them up to potential challenges or IP claims. The explanation points out that this shift contradicts the intent of patent law to protect the original inventor and exposes them to potential challenges. Scraped Law Snippet 2, § 8 PatG (Patentgesetz 1970), addresses compensation for inventions made by employees but implicitly supports the concept of inventor's rights, which the modified text undermines by allowing the disclosee to deduce some rights where they previously could not. This shift introduces ambiguity and potential conflict regarding ownership and rights related to confidential information.

perturbation.original_text: The supplier (supplier = contractor) assures that a ny and all necessary rights for the production and distribution of the furnished and delivered materia ls and products are available and holds Fronius harmless from all and any claims by third parties r esulting from an infringement of industrial and /or intellectual property rights/patents.
perturbation.changed_text: The supplier (supplier = contractor) assures that all necessary rights for the production and distribution of the furnished and delivered materials and products are available.
perturbation.explanation: Removing the phrase 'holds Fronius harmless from all and any claims by third parties resulting from an infringement of industrial and /or intellectual property rights/patents' weakens the protection for Fronius against IP infringement claims and potentially contradicts implied warranty provisions under Austrian commercial law.
perturbation.contradicted_law: Austrian General Civil Code (Allgemeines bürgerliches Gesetzbuch, ABGB)
perturbation.law_explanation: Austrian law (ABGB §922ff) implies a warranty that goods are free from defects, including intellectual property defects. Removing the 'hold harmless' clause means Fronius might not be protected from claims if the supplier's products infringe on third-party IP rights, even though the supplier warranted that they have the right to produce and distribute them. This contradicts the implicit protection granted by Austrian warranty law.

Er haftet also dafür, dass die Sache die bedungenen oder gewöhnlich vorausgesetzten Eigenschaften hat, dass sie seiner Beschreibung, einer Probe oder einem Muster entspricht und dass sie der Natur des Geschäftes oder der getroffenen Verabredung gemäß verwendet werden kann. (2) Absatz 2 Ob die Sache dem Vertrag entspricht, ist auch danach zu beurteilen, was der Übernehmer auf Grund der über sie gemachten öffentlichen Äußerungen des Übergebers oder des Herstellers, vor allem in der Werbung und in den der Sache beigefügten Angaben, erwarten kann; das gilt auch für öffentliche Äußerungen einer Person, die die Sache in den Europäisch
perturbation.justification: The modification removes the 'hold harmless' clause, weakening Fronius's protection against IP infringement claims. The Law Explanation suggests this contradicts Austrian warranty law (ABGB §922ff) which implies goods are free from defects, including intellectual property defects. Scraped Law Snippet 2, referencing § 922 ABGB, confirms this warranty, stating the seller is liable for ensuring the goods meet the agreed-upon characteristics. Removing the hold harmless clause means Fronius has less recourse if the supplier's products infringe on third-party IP rights, directly contradicting the protection offered by the implied warranty.

perturbation.original_text:
Jurisdiction is agreed to be at Wels
If any provision of this agreement is prohibited by law or judged by a court to be unlawful, void or unenforceable, the provision shall, to the extent r equired, be severed from this agreement and rendered ineffective as far as possible without mod ifying the remaining provisions of this agreement, and shall not in any way affect any other circumsta nces of or the validity or enforcement of this agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: Jurisdiction is agreed to be at Wels
perturbation.explanation: Removing the severability clause creates a risk that if one provision of the agreement is found unlawful, the entire agreement could be invalidated. This omission could contradict Austrian contract law principles that favor upholding contracts where possible.
perturbation.contradicted_law: Austrian General Civil Code (Allgemeines bürgerliches Gesetzbuch, ABGB)
perturbation.law_explanation: Austrian law, through ABGB § 878 and related principles, seeks to maintain the validity of contracts as much as possible. The deleted severability clause would normally ensure that an invalid provision doesn't invalidate the whole contract. Removing it contradicts the general intention of Austrian contract law to uphold agreements unless fundamental flaws make them entirely unenforceable.

Möglichkeit und Erlaubtheit - JUSLINE Österreich § 878 ABGB 3. Möglichkeit und Erlaubtheit ABGB - Allgemeines bürgerliches Gesetzbuch beobachten merken Berücksichtigter Stand der Gesetzgebung: 23.06.2025 § 878 . Paragraph 878, Was geradezu unmöglich ist, kann nicht Gegenstand eines gültigen Vertrages werden. Ist Mögliches und Unmögliches zugleich bedungen, so bleibt der Vertrag in ersterem Teile gültig, wenn anders aus dem Vertrage nicht hervorgeht, daß kein Punkt von dem anderen abgesondert werden könne. Wer bei Abschließung des Vertrages die Unmöglichkeit kannte oder kennen mußte, hat dem anderen Teile, falls von diesem nicht dasselbe gilt, den Schaden zu ersetzen, den er durch das Vertrauen auf die Gültigkeit des Vertrages erlitten hat. In Kraft seit 01.01.1917 bis 31.12.9999 merken 0 Kommentare zu § 878 ABGB Es sind keine Kommentare zu diesen Paragrafen vorhanden. Sie können den Inhalt von § 87
perturbation.justification: The removal of the severability clause directly contradicts the intent of Austrian law, specifically ABGB § 878, which seeks to uphold contracts as much as possible. The deleted clause would normally ensure that an invalid provision doesn't invalidate the whole contract. Removing this clause undermines the principle that contracts should be maintained unless fundamental flaws render them entirely unenforceable. The scraped snippet of § 878 ABGB states that if possible and impossible conditions are combined in a contract, the contract remains valid for the possible parts unless it's clear that the parts cannot be separated. The absence of a severability clause makes it harder to argue for the separation of valid and invalid parts, thus creating a contradiction.
